title: verbascum scrophulariifolium
intro: Verbascum scrophulariifolium, also known as Greek mullein, is a biennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its large, velvety leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum scrophulariifolium include soothing irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the removal of mucus from the lungs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Verbascum scrophulariifolium are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
